message:
fix(install): prevent false failure exit code when TMP_DIRS is empty

When TMP_DIRS is empty (e.g., when ntm is already installed and no temp
directories were created), the cleanup_tmp_dirs function would return
exit code 1, causing the installer to report failure despite successful
installation.

The issue occurs because:
1. "${TMP_DIRS[@]:-}" expands to a single empty string when array is empty
2. [ -n "" ] returns exit code 1
3. Due to set -euo pipefail, this propagates as the script's final exit code

Fix: Add `|| true` to absorb the failing test when iterating empty arrays.

Fixes: Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#52

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -29,7 +29,9 @@ TMP_DIRS=()
 cleanup_tmp_dirs() {
     local dir
     for dir in "${TMP_DIRS[@]:-}"; do
-        [ -n "$dir" ] && rm -rf "$dir"
+        # Use || true to prevent exit code 1 when TMP_DIRS is empty
+        # (empty string causes [ -n "" ] to fail, which propagates due to set -e)
+        [ -n "$dir" ] && rm -rf "$dir" || true
     done
 }
